title: The Role of Module Bundlers: Introduction to Webpack

questions.sentence: Modern frontend applications are composed of hundreds of different files, including JavaScript modules, CSS files, and images.
Webpack acts as a central hub that takes all these different files and their dependencies and bundles them together into a few optimized assets—usually a single JavaScript file and a single CSS file.

Beyond simple bundling, Webpack also performs tasks like 'Minification' (removing extra spaces to reduce file size) and 'Transpilation' (converting modern JavaScript into a version compatible with older browsers).
Understanding how bundlers work behind the scenes is crucial for optimizing your application's loading speed and ensuring a smooth deployment process.
